content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN small boxes arranged on a line from 111 to NNN. For each valid iii, the weight of the iii-th box is WiW_iWi​. Chef wants to bring them to his home, which is at the position 000. He can hold any number of boxes at the same time; however, the total weight of the boxes he's holding must not exceed K at any time, and he can only pick the ith box if all the boxes between Chef's home and the ith box have been either moved or picked up in this trip.

Therefore, Chef will pick up boxes and carry them home in one or more round trips. Find the smallest number of round trips he needs or determine that he cannot bring all boxes home.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K. The second line contains N space‑separated integers W1, W2, …, WN denoting the weights of the boxes from position 1 to N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of round trips required to bring all boxes home, or -1 if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: box weight 2 exceeds K=1 → impossible.
Test case 2: total weight 1+1=2 ≤ K=4, all boxes in one trip.
Test case 3: first trip takes box 1 (weight 3). Remaining boxes 4 and 2 fit together (3+4 would exceed K), so second trip takes both.
Test case 4: K=6 allows only one box per trip because any two consecutive boxes exceed the limit, thus three trips.

hidden.label: impossible because of a heavy box

solution.approach: The restriction that a box can be picked only after all boxes nearer to the home are already moved forces Chef to take boxes in order from the leftmost remaining box. In a single round trip Chef can therefore take a prefix of the remaining sequence whose total weight does not exceed K. To minimise the number of trips we should always take the longest possible prefix in each trip – any shorter choice would only increase the number of trips. The algorithm scans the array from left to right, accumulating a running sum. When adding the next weight would exceed K we finish the current trip, increment the answer, reset the sum and continue. If any single weight exceeds K the task is impossible and we output -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of boxes.
